title: My Hero Academia
opinion: grew up with it, post-war arc is peak, ending was mid but the journey was worth it
author: Kohei Horikoshi

power_system.ofa: One For All: stockpiling quirk, 9 users. Sub-quirks: Blackwhip, Float, Danger Sense, Smokescreen, Fa Jin, Gear Shift
power_system.afo: All For One: steals/gives quirks, main villain

class_1a.Deku: Izuku Midoriya, OFA user, was quirkless, protagonist
class_1a.Bakugo: Katsuki, explosion quirk, rival, died and came back in final war
class_1a.Todoroki: Shoto, half-cold half-hot, Endeavor's son, daddy issues
class_1a.Iida: Tenya, engine legs, class rep, Ingenium

villains.All For One: Ancient villain, steals quirks, manipulated Shigaraki
villains.Shigaraki: Tenko Shimura, decay, AFO's successor, tragic backstory
villains.Dabi: Toya Todoroki, blue flames, Endeavor's son, reveal was massive
villains.Stain: Hero Killer, only respects All Might, paralysis via blood

hot_takes: Post-war arc is peak MHA, anyone who says sports festival is nostalgic not correct
Ending was rushed, Deku losing powers felt hollow
Dabi reveal was peak but his fight was underwhelming
Horikoshi was clearly exhausted by the end
